mapping.b_element: fast per-frame memory application via cross-view attention

mapping.a_element: slow-timescale evolution of the fitness landscape matrix
two-timescale separation of fast and slow variables
mapping.b_element: decoupling of the update frequency from the application frequency

brief_justification: The only genuine overlap is the generic design pattern of separating a fast process from a slow one; the actual machinery differs. Paper A's core is a Tikhonov singular-perturbation reduction to a quasi-steady state followed by maximizing mean fitness over a convex set via linear programming, with an error-catastrophe-like threshold. Paper B has no steady-state reduction, no optimization functional, and no threshold: it is a heuristic frequency-decoupling of gradient updates with attention-based warping and anti-drift regularization losses, so no role-for-role mapping of the operative mechanism holds.
